card_page.policy_text: --> Get a Library Card! | maynardpubliclibrary top of page CALL THE LIBRARY DIRECTIONS TO LIBRARY EMAIL SUPPORT EMAIL NEWSLETTER CATALOG MY ACCOUNT UPCOMING EVENTS MUSEUM PASSES BOOK A ROOM MOBILE PRINTING HOME Events YouTube, Netflix, & Streaming Options Film: The Terminal Bucky Lew - Basketball's First Black Pro How to Live on Mars (and not die) Film: Big Boys Maynard's Motion Picture History Understanding AI and Misinformation Job Seekers Group Cookbook Club Spice Club Book Mill Book Group MPL Silent Book Club Art Talks with Jane Oneail Recorded Local History Presentations Past Virtual Events Services Access to Justice Computers, Printing, Copying, Faxing English Conversation Classes Exam Proctoring Interlibrary Loan Homebound Delivery Library of Things Local History Collection Maynard Links Mobile Printing Periodicals Reader's Advisory Subject Resource Guide Tax Forms/Tax Assistance E-Resources Children Welcome to Childrens Children's Summer Reading Club E-Books Children's E-mail List StoryWalk Teens Teen Summer Library Club E-Books Library Info Directions Get a Library Card! Library Policies and Plans Library History & Photos Library Staff & Trustees List of Minuteman Libraries Room Reservations Exhibits at the Library Museum Passes Strategic Plan Strategic Plan Infographics Suggest New Item for Purchase Library Friends Join or donate! Visiting Authors Book Sales Donate Books, CDs, & DVDs More Use tab to navigate through the menu items. Sunday Monday Tuesday Wednesday Thursday Friday Saturday Closed 10am - 5pm 2pm - 9pm 10am - 5pm 2pm - 9pm 10am - 5pm 10am - 5pm Library Card Information Library cards are free! They are quick and easy to get, with no waiting period. ​ Any resident of Massachusetts, age 4 or older, who can provide an ID proving current address is entitled to a library card. Children (4-13 years old) need a parent or guardian’s signature and ID to obtain a card. There is no replacement charge for a library card. Lost cards should be reported immediately. You must present your card or a photo ID at the Circulation Desk to check out library materials. Library cards may be used at any of the Minuteman Library Network (MLN) libraries. (There are 41 MLN libraries as of October 2024.) Your library card number and a PIN are required for online access to your account. ​ Policy approved by Board of Library Trustees, 17 May 2022 Don't have a card yet, but need access right now? We've got you covered! You can apply for a library eCard from the Maynard Public Library online! The library is part of a consortium of libraries known as the Minuteman Library Network. This network makes it possible for you to complete a form online to create a Minuteman library eCard. What are the benefits? You can use this eCard to: Login to My Account in the Minuteman catalog and establish a PIN. Reserve items in the Minuteman catalog . Use state-provided online research databases within Massachusetts only. Access ebooks and audio in our Minuteman digital collections . Use Maynard digital collections if you reside in Maynard. To access the online registration form, click here. To get a physical library card , which provides in-library borrowing privileges, visit the Maynard Public Library in person. Click here for directions to our building , as well as our hours of operation. Important notes: When visiting the library, please mention that you have already registered for an eCard and bring your card number. ​ Already have a card but need to renew it? ​ You can renew your card either by visiting the circulation desk at the library or by using this online renewal form . ​ Questions? Call the library at 978-897-1010 or email maycirc@minlib.net . Maynard Public Library ▪ 77 Nason Street ▪ Maynard, MA 01754 ▪ Phone 978-897-1010 bottom of page

pass_page.policy_text:
To limit museum types being displayed, check the corresponding categories:
To select a museum, click “Details/Reserve”, then select which Pass to reserve.
{"@context": "http://schema.org","@type": "CivicStructure","address": "568 Main Street, Hudson, MA 01749","logo": "https://s3.amazonaws.com/ai-assets/museum-logos/ma-american-heritage-museum_150_md.jpg","telephone": "978-562-9182","name": "American Heritage Museum","url": "","description": "The American Heritage Museum, located in Hudson, MA, is dedicated to military history. Exhibits examine conflicts from the Revolutionary War through 9/11 and the &ldquo;War on Terror.&rdquo; A good portion of the museum is focused on the sizable Littlefield collection, which includes many rare items from the U.S. and other countries. Among other artifacts, visitors to the museum will encounter aircraft, tanks, guns, and automobiles, some dating from the beginning of the 20th century."} American Heritage Museum 568 Main Street, Hudson, MA 01749978-562-9182https://www.americanheritagemuseum.org/[Details/Reserve]
American Heritage Museum The American Heritage Museum, located in Hudson, MA, is dedicated to military history. Exhibits examine conflicts from the Revolutionary War through 9/11 and the “War on Terror.” A good portion of the museum is focused on the sizable Littlefield collection, which includes many rare items from the U.S. and other countries. Among other artifacts, visitors to the museum will encounter aircraft, tanks, guns, and automobiles, some dating from the beginning of the 20th century.
Categories: Family, History, Military
Pass Type: Circulating Pass (must be picked up and returned to the branch)
Pass Benefits Admits up to 6 (adults or children) at half price per person. Valid for admission only. Not valid for special event weekends or driving or ride experiences. Advanced purchase of tickets on-line is suggested but, tickets can be purchased in person.
Reserve a Pass Reserve a Pass
{"@context": "http://schema.org","@type": "CivicStructure","address": "308 Congress Street, Boston, MA 02210","logo": "https://s3.amazonaws.com/ai-assets/museum-logos/ma-boston-childrens-museum_3_md.jpg","telephone": "617-426-6500","name": "Boston Children&apos;s Museum","url": "","description": "Founded in 1913 by the Science Teachers&apos; Bureau, the Boston Children&apos;s Museum is the second oldest children&apos;s museum in the world and one of the few that maintain a collection. Designed for both children and families, the Museum exhibits focus on science, culture, environmental awareness, health & fitness, and the arts, emphasizing hands-on engagement and learning through experience, and employing play as a tool to spark the inherent creativity, curiosity, and imagination of children."} Boston Children's Museum 308 Congress Street, Boston, MA 02210617-426-6500http://www.bostonchildrensmuseum.org/[Details/Reserve]
Boston Children's Museum Founded in 1913 by the Science Teachers' Bureau, the Boston Children's Museum is the second oldest children's museum in the world and one of the few that maintain a collection. Designed for both children and families, the Museum exhibits focus on science, culture, environmental awareness, health & fitness, and the arts, emphasizing hands-on engagement and learning through experience, and employing play as a tool to spark the inherent creativity, curiosity, and imagination of children.
Categories: Children, Crafts, Family, Technology
Pass Type: Printable/Digital Coupon Pass (link delivered by email)
Pass Benefits The Boston Children's Museum pass offers half price admission for up to four (4) people.
{"@context": "http://schema.org","@type": "CivicStructure","address": "15 State Street, Suite 1100, Boston, MA 02109","logo": "https://s3.amazonaws.com/ai-assets/museum-logos/ma-boston-harbor-islands_6_md.jpg","telephone": "617-223-8666","name": "Boston Harbor Island Ferry","url": "","description": "The largest recreational open space in Eastern Massachusetts, the Boston Harbor Islands National and State Park is comprised of 34 islands and mainland parks that deliver access to historic sites and provide opportunities to hike and camp. Transportation to the islands is by ferry and, depending on the location, your own boat. Please refer to their website for the most up-to-date information about what is open and how to get there."} Boston Harbor Island Ferry 15 State Street, Suite 1100, Boston, MA 02109617-223-8666http://www.bostonharborislands.org/[Details/Reserve]
Boston Harbor Island Ferry The largest recreational open space in Eastern Massachusetts, the Boston Harbor Islands National and State Park is comprised of 34 islands and mainland parks that deliver access to historic sites and provide opportunities to hike and camp. Transportation to the islands is by ferry and, depending on the location, your own boat. Please refer to their website for the most up-to-date information about what is open and how to get there.
Categories: Family, Nature, Recreation
Pass Benefits PLEASE NOTE: This is an electronic code that you will receive via email. You must purchase your tickets through the institution before your visit.
This library pass discounts tickets at a 2-for-1 rate. You can buy up to 4 tickets for the price of just 2.
Weekdays in late May to early October. Ferry from Boston to Spectacle Island runs Thursday through Sunday in the fall.
Summer Schedule: June 20 – September 2
Boston to Spectacle Island: Monday through Friday
Boston to Georges Island: Monday through Friday
Hingham to Peddocks Island: Monday through Friday
Fall Schedule: September 5 – September 22
Boston to Georges Island: Thursday through Sunday
End of Season Schedule: September 27 – October 11
Boston to Georges Island: Friday through Sunday
{"@context": "http://schema.org","@type": "CivicStructure","address": "200 Lexington Road, Concord, MA 01742","logo": "https://s3.amazonaws.com/ai-assets/museum-logos/ma-concord-museum_10_md.png","telephone": "978-369-9763","name": "Concord Museum","url": "","description": "Founded in 1886, the Concord Museum is a repository of local history and a gateway to the town of Concord. It educates visitors from around the world about the history of Concord and its continuing influence on American political, literary and cultural life through permanent and changing exhibits, educational programs and publications. Its collection contains a range of unique items from the area, including a Paul Revere lantern; many objects that once belonged to Henry David Thoreau; a reproduction of Ralph Waldo Emerson&apos;s study; and furniture, artwork, and objects from the colonial era on. "} Concord Museum 200 Lexington Road, Concord, MA 01742978-369-9763http://www.concordmuseum.org/[Details/Reserve]
Concord Museum Founded in 1886, the Concord Museum is a repository of local history and a gateway to the town of Concord. It educates visitors from around the world about the history of Concord and its continuing influence on American political, literary and cultural life through permanent and changing exhibits, educational programs and publications. Its collection contains a range of unique items from the area, including a Paul Revere lantern; many objects that once belonged to Henry David Thoreau; a reproduction of Ralph Waldo Emerson's study; and furniture, artwork, and objects from the colonial era on.
Categories: Family, History
